LAW AND MOTION, 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE IMPLIED WARRANTY CLAIMS IN THE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. ALL REQUESTS FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE ARE GRANTED. THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ("FAC") PLEADS THAT THE DEFECT EXISTED AT THE TIME OF SALE, AND THUS WITHIN THE ONE-YEAR IMPLIED WARRANTY DURATION PERIOD PRESCRIBED BY THE SONG-BEVERLY ACT. (FAC, 7.) THE FAC ALSO PLEADS THAT THE LATENT DEFECT COULD NOT HAVE BEEN REASONABLY DISCOVERED UNTIL AUGUST 18, 2011 BECAUSE DEFENDANT PERFORMED "BAND-AID REPAIRS" TO COVER UP THE ALLEGED DEFECT. (FAC, 32, 39, 143-154.) THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS WAS TOLLED UNTIL PLAINTIFF COULD HAVE REASONABLY DISCOVERED THE LATENT DEFECT, AND THE COMPLAINT IS THEREFORE TIMELY. (SEE MEXIA V. RINKER BOAT CO. INC. ((2009) 174 CAL.APP.4TH 1297, 1304.) (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT HEARING SET FOR FEB-26-2014 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO CAUSES OF ACTION ONE THROUGH FIVE TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEFENDANT'S REQUESTS FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE ARE GRANTED. THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ("FAC") ADEQUATELY PLEADS FACTS ESTABLISHING DELAYED DISCOVERY AND IS THUS NOT BARRED BY THE APPLICABLE STATUTES OF LIMITATION. ALTHOUGH THE FAC ALLEGES THAT THE TRUCK WAS DELIVERED WITH "SERIOUS DEFECTS" (FAC, 7), IT ALSO ALLEGES THAT THESE DEFECTS WERE NOT DISCOVERED UNTIL AUGUST 18, 2011 BECAUSE DEFENDANT PERFORMED "BAND-AID REPAIRS" TO COVER UP THE ALLEGED DEFECTS. (FAC, 32, 39, 143-154.) AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND, AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION, THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. THE FAC ALLEGES INTENTIONAL TORTIOUS CONDUCT INDEPENDENT OF A BREACH OF CONTRACT. THE FAC ALLEGES THAT DEFENDANTS REPRESENTED TO PLAINTIFF ON MULTIPLE OCCASIONS THAT THEY COULD FIX THE DEFECTS WITH THE ENGINE, EVEN THOUGH THEY KNEW THERE WAS A MORE SERIOUS PROBLEM THAT COULD ONLY BE REMEDIED BY REPLACEMENT. (FAC, 25, 143-154.) EACH TIME DEFENDANT MISREPRESENTED THAT IT COULD REPAIR THE TRUCK, WHICH IT KNEW IT COULD NOT DO, IT INTENTIONALLY COMMITTED TORTIOUS ACTS INDEPENDENT OF THE CONTRACT. (SEE ROBINSON HELICOPTER CO. V. DANA CORP. (2004) 34 CAL.4TH 979, 991; UNITED GUAR. MORTG. INDEMN. CO V. COUNTRYWIDE FINANCIAL CORP. (C.D. CAL. 2009) 660 F.SUPP.2D 1163, 1181.) AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. (SEE ROBINSON HELICOPTER, 34 CAL.4TH AT 990.) AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION, THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. THE FAC ADEQUATELY PLEADS VIOLATION OF THE CONSUMER LEGAL REMEDIES ACT. (FAC, 14-15, 17, 278, 280, 287.) (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-21-2014 Set For Trial On Oct-20-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Defendant Ford Motor Company'S Motion To Strike 1st Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Feb-26-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendant Ford Motor Company'S Motion To Strike the implied warranty claims in the 1st Amended Complaint is Denied. All requests for judicial notice are granted. The First Amended Complaint ("FAC") pleads that the defect existed at the time of sale, and thus within the one-year implied warranty duration period prescribed by the Song-Beverly Act. (FAC, 7.) The FAC also pleads that the latent defect could not have been reasonably discovered until August 18, 2011 because Defendant performed "band-aid repairs" to cover up the alleged defect. (FAC, 32, 39, 143-154.) The statute of limitations was tolled until Plaintiff could have reasonably discovered the latent defect, and the complaint is therefore timely. (See Mexia v. Rinker Boat Co. Inc. ((2009) 174 Cal.App.4th 1297, 1304.) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)

Defendant Ford Motor Company'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Feb-26-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendant Ford Motor Company'S Demurrer to Causes of action one through five To 1st Amended Complaint is sustained without leave to amend in part and overruled in part. Defendant's requests for judicial notice are granted. The First Amended Complaint ("FAC") adequately pleads facts establishing delayed discovery and is thus not barred by the applicable statutes of limitation. Although the FAC alleges that the truck was delivered with "serious defects" (FAC, 7), it also alleges that these defects were not discovered until August 18, 2011 because Defendant performed "band-aid repairs" to cover up the alleged defects. (FAC, 32, 39, 143-154.) As to the first, second, and fourth causes of action, the demurrer is overruled. The FAC alleges intentional tortious conduct independent of a breach of contract. The FAC alleges that Defendants represented to Plaintiff on multiple occasions that they could fix the defects with the engine, even though they knew there was a more serious problem that could only be remedied by replacement. (FAC, 25, 143-154.) Each time Defendant misrepresented that it could repair the truck, which it knew it could not do, it intentionally committed tortious acts independent of the contract. (See Robinson Helicopter Co. v. Dana Corp. (2004) 34 Cal.4th 979, 991; United Guar. Mortg. Indemn. Co v. Countrywide Financial Corp. (C.D. Cal. 2009) 660 F.Supp.2d 1163, 1181.) As to the third cause of action for negligent misrepresentation, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. (See Robinson Helicopter, 34 Cal.4th at 990.) As to the fifth cause of action, the demurrer is overruled. The FAC adequately pleads violation of the Consumer Legal Remedies Act. (FAC, 14-15, 17, 278, 280, 287.) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)
